title: Bidirectional vs. Unidirectional Context
concept: BERT sees the whole sentence at once, making it better for extracting meaning; GPT only sees the past, making it better for generating text.

exercises.prompt: What does "context direction" mean in AI?

options.text: How AI models look at surrounding words
How fast the AI can download data
Whether the AI uses English or French

exercises.explain: Some AI models read in one direction to predict what comes next, while others look at the entire sentence at once to understand the context.

cards.title: Looking Both Ways
cards.body: Have you ever tried to guess the end of a sentence before someone finishes speaking? That is how some AIs read! Other AIs wait to see the whole sentence before deciding what it means. Let's see why this difference matters.

cards.title: Two Ways to Read
cards.body: Unidirectional models (like GPT) only look at past words to predict what comes next. Bidirectional models (like BERT) look at the words before AND after a target word at the same time to understand its full meaning.

cards.title: The Reader vs. The Writer
cards.body: Think of a reader versus a writer. BERT is like a reader looking at an entire finished sentence on a page all at once. GPT is like a writer typing out a message word by word, only able to see what they have already written.

cards.title: The Bank Example
cards.body:
In the sentence: "The bank of the river was muddy." 

BERT looks at both 'bank' and 'river' at the same time to know 'bank' means land, not a place with money. GPT reads 'The bank of...' and has to guess what kind of bank it is before it even sees the word 'river'!

cards.title: Is Both Ways Always Better?
cards.body: People think looking in both directions is always better. But if an AI can see the future words, it can easily cheat when practicing writing! That is why text generators like GPT are designed to only look backward.

cards.title: Direction Comparison
cards.body: Depending on what you want the AI to do, you choose either one-way or two-way vision.

right.text: Looks at past and future. Perfect for analyzing and understanding text.

left.text: Looks only at the past. Perfect for generating new text word-by-word.

exercises.prompt: Why is GPT considered a "unidirectional" model?

options.text: It looks at the beginning and the end at the same time
It only looks at the words that came before the current word
It reads the sentence backward from right to left
It skips every other word to read faster

exercises.explain: GPT is designed to predict the very next word based only on the words that came before it, which is why it is unidirectional.

exercises.prompt: BERT is bidirectional, meaning it can look at words to the left and to the right at the same time.
exercises.explain: BERT is bidirectional, meaning it can analyze words on both the left and right sides of any word.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the explanation of BERT's reading style.
exercises.sentence: BERT uses [blank] context to look at the entire sentence at the same time.
exercises.explain: Because BERT is bidirectional, it can look at the words before and after to get the full picture.

exercises.prompt: Which of these tasks is best suited for a unidirectional model like GPT?

options.text: Finding the name of a city in a paragraph
Writing a creative poem from scratch
Deciding if a movie review is happy or sad

exercises.explain: Text generation requires predicting the next word step-by-step, which is best done by a unidirectional model like GPT. The other two tasks require analyzing existing text.

exercises.prompt: Which statement best describes the differences between BERT and GPT?

options.text: BERT reads only left-to-right to generate new sentences
BERT looks at the whole sentence to understand it, while GPT looks backward to write the next word
GPT looks forward into the future to correct past spelling mistakes
Both models must read sentences backward first to understand human language

exercises.explain: BERT's ability to read in both directions makes it excellent at finding answers and understanding meaning, while GPT's one-direction design makes it natural at writing new text.
